findings.description: The round-1 regression test `tests/harnesses/test_deep_agents_astream.py::test_thread_id_unchanged_after_imports` (intended to lock gemini #5 — `create_agent` no longer overwrites `self._thread_id`) does not actually exercise `create_agent`. The fixture `_make_harness()` uses `DeepAgentsHarness.__new__` to skip `__init__` and then sets `_thread_id` manually; the test then only reads the `thread_id` property repeatedly. If a future change re-introduced `self._thread_id = str(uuid4())` inside `create_agent` (the exact regression the test name claims to guard), the test would still pass because `create_agent` is never invoked. The underlying source fix is correct — `src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/deep_agents.py:65-92` does not touch `_thread_id` — but the lock-in is illusory.
findings.resolution: Strengthen the regression so it would fail if `create_agent` were reverted. One approach: construct a harness via the real `__init__` (capture the synthesized thread_id), then monkey-patch the deepagents.create_deep_agent / init_chat_model / InMemorySaver / compose_system_prompt names to no-op stubs, call `await harness.create_agent(tools=[], extensions=[])`, and assert `harness.thread_id == initial`. Alternative: introspect `DeepAgentsHarness.create_agent` source via `inspect.getsource(DeepAgentsHarness.create_agent)` and assert it does not contain `self._thread_id =` — uglier but cheap and 100% revert-detecting.

findings.description: The round-1 gemini #2 fix in `ms_agent_fw.py` uses a single `pending_orphan_call_id` slot to correlate missing-id `function_result` with a prior missing-id `function_call`. For SDK-conformant streams (call_id always present) this is irrelevant. For sequential missing-id calls it works. For *parallel/interleaved* missing-id calls (start A → start B → result A → result B), the second start overwrites the slot, so result A pairs with B's synth UUID and result B falls through to a fresh UUID — both end up mis-bracketed. The inline comment acknowledges 'best-effort for sequential tool calls' and the scenario violates the SDK contract. Flagged as a documented limitation, not a blocking bug: in v1 single-user-loopback scope with the official agent-framework SDK, this code path is unreachable. No fix needed for round 2.

The FastAPI lifespan (`src/assistant/web/app.py:142-173`) gates `httpx.AsyncClient` creation on `getattr(pc, 'tool_sources', None) or {}`. In production this is correct. In the web/integration tests (`tests/web/test_app.py:_make_app_with_harness`, `tests/integration/test_ag_ui_smoke.py:_make_app`), `pc` is a `MagicMock`, whose auto-attribute lookup `pc.tool_sources` returns a truthy `MagicMock` instead of a falsy dict. As a result every test that uses `_make_app_with_harness` now constructs a real `httpx.AsyncClient` per request and aclose()s it in the finally — wasteful but harmless (no network I/O, no leaked file descriptors because aclose runs). No test regression; flagging because the fix delta introduced unnecessary work in the test path.
